lines.gurmukhi: ਨਾਨਕ ਨਾਮਿ ਰਤੇ ਜਨ ਨਿਰਮਲੇ ਸਹਜੇ ਸਚਿ ਸਮਾਉ ॥੨॥
lines.translit: naanak naam rate jan niramale sahaje sach samaau |2|
lines.english: O Nanak, those humble beings who are imbued with the Naam are pure and immaculate. They are intuitively merged in the True Lord. ||2||

lines.gurmukhi: ਗੁਰਮੁਖਿ ਸਬਦੁ ਸਮੑਾਲੀਐ ਸਚੇ ਕੇ ਗੁਣ ਗਾਉ ॥
lines.translit: guramukh sabad samaaleeai sache ke gun gaau |
lines.english: The Gurmukhs dwell on the Word of the Shabad. They sing the Glorious Praises of the True Lord.

lines.gurmukhi: ਪਉੜੀ ॥
lines.translit: paurree |
lines.english: Pauree:

lines.gurmukhi: ਪੂਰਾ ਸਤਿਗੁਰੁ ਸੇਵਿ ਪੂਰਾ ਪਾਇਆ ॥
lines.translit: pooraa satigur sev pooraa paaeaa |
lines.english: Serving the Perfect True Guru, I have found the Perfect Lord.

lines.gurmukhi: ਪੂਰੈ ਕਰਮਿ ਧਿਆਇ ਪੂਰਾ ਸਬਦੁ ਮੰਨਿ ਵਸਾਇਆ ॥
lines.translit: poorai karam dhiaae pooraa sabad man vasaaeaa |
lines.english: Meditating on the Perfect Lord, by perfect karma, I have enshrined the Shabad within my mind.

lines.gurmukhi: ਪੂਰੈ ਗਿਆਨਿ ਧਿਆਨਿ ਮੈਲੁ ਚੁਕਾਇਆ ॥
lines.translit: poorai giaan dhiaan mail chukaaeaa |
lines.english: Through perfect spiritual wisdom and meditation, my filth has been washed away.

lines.gurmukhi: ਹਰਿ ਸਰਿ ਤੀਰਥਿ ਜਾਣਿ ਮਨੂਆ ਨਾਇਆ ॥
lines.translit: har sar teerath jaan manooaa naaeaa |
lines.english: The Lord is my sacred shrine of pilgrimage and pool of purification; I wash my mind in Him.

lines.gurmukhi: ਪੁਛਿ ਨ ਸਕੈ ਕੋਇ ਜਾਂ ਖਸਮੈ ਭਾਇਆ ॥
lines.translit: puchh na sakai koe jaan khasamai bhaaeaa |
lines.english: No one can challenge that person, with whom our Lord and Master is pleased.

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਬਦਿ ਮਰੈ ਮਨੁ ਮਾਰਿ ਧੰਨੁ ਜਣੇਦੀ ਮਾਇਆ ॥
lines.translit: sabad marai man maar dhan janedee maaeaa |
lines.english: One who dies in the Shabad and conquers his mind - blessed is the mother who gave birth to him.

lines.gurmukhi: ਦਰਿ ਸਚੈ ਸਚਿਆਰੁ ਸਚਾ ਆਇਆ ॥
lines.translit: dar sachai sachiaar sachaa aaeaa |
lines.english: He is true in the Court of the Lord, and his coming into this world is judged to be true.

lines.gurmukhi: ਕੁਲਹਾਂ ਦੇਂਦੇ ਬਾਵਲੇ ਲੈਂਦੇ ਵਡੇ ਨਿਲਜ ॥
lines.translit: kulahaan dende baavale lainde vadde nilaj |
lines.english: Those who give out ceremonial hats of recognition are fools; those who receive them have no shame.

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਲੋਕ ਮਃ ੧ ॥
lines.translit: salok mahalaa 1 |
lines.english: Salok, Third Mehl:

lines.gurmukhi: ਨਾਨਕ ਸਚੁ ਸਲਾਹਿ ਲਿਖਿਆ ਪਾਇਆ ॥੧੮॥
lines.translit: naanak sach salaeh likhiaa paaeaa |18|
lines.english: O Nanak, praising the True Lord, his pre-ordained destiny is activated. ||18||

lines.gurmukhi: ਦੇਨੑਿ ਦੁਆਈ ਸੇ ਮਰਹਿ ਜਿਨ ਕਉ ਦੇਨਿ ਸਿ ਜਾਹਿ ॥
lines.translit: dena duaaee se mareh jin kau den si jaeh |
lines.english: Those who give out blessings shall die, and those that they bless shall also depart.

lines.gurmukhi: ਚੂਹਾ ਖਡ ਨ ਮਾਵਈ ਤਿਕਲਿ ਬੰਨੑੈ ਛਜ ॥
lines.translit: choohaa khadd na maavee tikal banai chhaj |
lines.english: The mouse cannot enter its hole with a basket tied around its waist.

lines.gurmukhi: ਫਸਲਿ ਅਹਾੜੀ ਏਕੁ ਨਾਮੁ ਸਾਵਣੀ ਸਚੁ ਨਾਉ ॥
lines.translit: fasal ahaarree ek naam saavanee sach naau |
lines.english: The spring harvest is the Name of the One Lord; the harvest of autumn is the True Name.

lines.gurmukhi: ਨਾਨਕ ਹੁਕਮੁ ਨ ਜਾਪਈ ਕਿਥੈ ਜਾਇ ਸਮਾਹਿ ॥
lines.translit: naanak hukam na jaapee kithai jaae samaeh |
lines.english: O Nanak, no one knows the Lord's Command, by which all must depart.

lines.gurmukhi: ਦੁਨੀਆ ਕੇ ਦਰ ਕੇਤੜੇ ਕੇਤੇ ਆਵਹਿ ਜਾਂਹਿ ॥
lines.translit: duneea ke dar ketarre kete aaveh jaanhi |
lines.english: There are so many courts of the world, and so many who come and go there.

lines.gurmukhi: ਮੈ ਮਹਦੂਦੁ ਲਿਖਾਇਆ ਖਸਮੈ ਕੈ ਦਰਿ ਜਾਇ ॥
lines.translit: mai mahadood likhaaeaa khasamai kai dar jaae |
lines.english: I receive a letter of pardon from my Lord and Master, when I reach His Court.

lines.gurmukhi: ਕੇਤੇ ਮੰਗਹਿ ਮੰਗਤੇ ਕੇਤੇ ਮੰਗਿ ਮੰਗਿ ਜਾਹਿ ॥੧॥
lines.translit: kete mangeh mangate kete mang mang jaeh |1|
lines.english: There are so many beggars begging; so many beg and beg until death. ||1||

lines.translit: mahalaa 1 |
lines.english: First Mehl:

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਉ ਮਣੁ ਹਸਤੀ ਘਿਉ ਗੁੜੁ ਖਾਵੈ ਪੰਜਿ ਸੈ ਦਾਣਾ ਖਾਇ ॥
lines.translit: sau man hasatee ghiau gurr khaavai panj sai daanaa khaae |
lines.english: The elephant eats a hundred pounds of ghee and molasses, and five hundred pounds of corn.

lines.gurmukhi: ਖਸਮਿ ਮਿਟੀ ਫਿਰਿ ਭਾਨੀ ॥
lines.translit: khasam mittee fir bhaanee |
lines.english: Submitting to the Lord, one become pleasing to Him.

lines.gurmukhi: ਡਕੈ ਫੂਕੈ ਖੇਹ ਉਡਾਵੈ ਸਾਹਿ ਗਇਐ ਪਛੁਤਾਇ ॥
lines.translit: ddakai fookai kheh uddaavai saeh geiai pachhutaae |
lines.english: He belches and grunts and scatters dust, and when the breath leaves his body, he regrets it.

lines.gurmukhi: ਅੰਧੀ ਫੂਕਿ ਮੁਈ ਦੇਵਾਨੀ ॥
lines.translit: andhee fook muee devaanee |
lines.english: The blind and arrogant die insane.

lines.gurmukhi: ਖਸਮੈ ਭਾਵੈ ਓਹਾ ਚੰਗੀ ਜਿ ਕਰੇ ਖੁਦਾਇ ਖੁਦਾਇ ॥
lines.translit: khasamai bhaavai ohaa changee ji kare khudaae khudaae |
lines.english: The good sparrow is pleasing to her Lord and Master, if she chirps the Name of the Lord.

lines.gurmukhi: ਅਧੁ ਗੁਲ੍ਹਾ ਚਿੜੀ ਕਾ ਚੁਗਣੁ ਗੈਣਿ ਚੜੀ ਬਿਲਲਾਇ ॥
lines.translit: adh gulhaa chirree kaa chugan gain charree bilalaae |
lines.english: The sparrow eats only half a grain, then it flies through the sky and chirps.

lines.gurmukhi: ਹੋਇ ਸਤਾਣਾ ਘੁਰੈ ਨ ਮਾਵੈ ਸਾਹਿ ਗਇਐ ਪਛੁਤਾਇ ॥
lines.translit: hoe sataanaa ghurai na maavai saeh geiai pachhutaae |
lines.english: It becomes very strong, and cannot be contained in its den, but when it must go, it regrets.

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਕਤਾ ਸੀਹੁ ਮਾਰੇ ਸੈ ਮਿਰਿਆ ਸਭ ਪਿਛੈ ਪੈ ਖਾਇ ॥
lines.translit: sakataa seehu maare sai miriaa sabh pichhai pai khaae |
lines.english: The powerful tiger kills hundreds of deer, and all sorts of other animals eat what it leaves.

lines.gurmukhi: ਅਕ ਸਿਉ ਪ੍ਰੀਤਿ ਕਰੇ ਅਕ ਤਿਡਾ ਅਕ ਡਾਲੀ ਬਹਿ ਖਾਇ ॥
lines.translit: ak siau preet kare ak tiddaa ak ddaalee beh khaae |
lines.english: The insect loves the milkweed plant; perched on its branch, it eats it.

lines.gurmukhi: ਅੰਧਾ ਕਿਸ ਨੋ ਬੁਕਿ ਸੁਣਾਵੈ ॥
lines.translit: andhaa kis no buk sunaavai |
lines.english: So who is impressed by the roar of the blind beast?

lines.gurmukhi: ਖਸਮੈ ਮੂਲਿ ਨ ਭਾਵੈ ॥
lines.translit: khasamai mool na bhaavai |
lines.english: He is not pleasing at all to his Lord and Master.

lines.gurmukhi: ਨਾਨਕ ਦੁਨੀਆ ਚਾਰਿ ਦਿਹਾੜੇ ਸੁਖਿ ਕੀਤੈ ਦੁਖੁ ਹੋਈ ॥
lines.translit: naanak duneea chaar dihaarre sukh keetai dukh hoee |
lines.english: O Nanak, the world lasts for only a few days; indulging in pleasures, pain is produced.

lines.gurmukhi: ਖਸਮੈ ਭਾਵੈ ਓਹੋ ਚੰਗਾ ਜਿ ਕਰੇ ਖੁਦਾਇ ਖੁਦਾਇ ॥
lines.translit: khasamai bhaavai oho changaa ji kare khudaae khudaae |
lines.english: It becomes good and pleasing to its Lord and Master, if it chirps the Name of the Lord.

lines.gurmukhi: ਮਖਂ‍ੀ ਮਿਠੈ ਮਰਣਾ ॥
lines.translit: makhanee mitthai maranaa |
lines.english: The fly dies for the sake of sweets.

lines.gurmukhi: ਜਿਨ ਤੂ ਰਖਹਿ ਤਿਨ ਨੇੜਿ ਨ ਆਵੈ ਤਿਨ ਭਉ ਸਾਗਰੁ ਤਰਣਾ ॥੨॥
lines.translit: jin too rakheh tin nerr na aavai tin bhau saagar taranaa |2|
lines.english: O Lord, death does not even approach those whom You protect. You carry them across the terrifying world-ocean. ||2||

lines.gurmukhi: ਗਲਾ ਵਾਲੇ ਹੈਨਿ ਘਣੇਰੇ ਛਡਿ ਨ ਸਕੈ ਕੋਈ ॥
lines.translit: galaa vaale hain ghanere chhadd na sakai koee |
lines.english: There are many who boast and brag, but none of them can remain detached from the world.

lines.gurmukhi: ਪਉੜੀ ॥
lines.translit: paurree |
lines.english: Pauree:

lines.gurmukhi: ਅਗਮ ਅਗੋਚਰੁ ਤੂ ਧਣੀ ਸਚਾ ਅਲਖ ਅਪਾਰੁ ॥
lines.translit: agam agochar too dhanee sachaa alakh apaar |
lines.english: You are Inaccessible and Unfathomable, O Invisible and Infinite True Lord Master.

lines.gurmukhi: ਤੂ ਦਾਤਾ ਸਭਿ ਮੰਗਤੇ ਇਕੋ ਦੇਵਣਹਾਰੁ ॥
lines.translit: too daataa sabh mangate iko devanahaar |
lines.english: You are the Giver, all are beggars of You. You alone are the Great Giver.

lines.gurmukhi: ਜਿਨੀ ਸੇਵਿਆ ਤਿਨੀ ਸੁਖੁ ਪਾਇਆ ਗੁਰਮਤੀ ਵੀਚਾਰੁ ॥
lines.translit: jinee seviaa tinee sukh paaeaa guramatee veechaar |
lines.english: Those who serve You find peace, reflecting on the Guru's Teachings.

lines.gurmukhi: ਇਕਨਾ ਨੋ ਤੁਧੁ ਏਵੈ ਭਾਵਦਾ ਮਾਇਆ ਨਾਲਿ ਪਿਆਰੁ ॥
lines.translit: eikanaa no tudh evai bhaavadaa maaeaa naal piaar |
lines.english: Some, according to Your Will, are in love with Maya.

lines.gurmukhi: ਵਿਣੁ ਪ੍ਰੀਤੀ ਭਗਤਿ ਨ ਹੋਵਈ ਵਿਣੁ ਸਤਿਗੁਰ ਨ ਲਗੈ ਪਿਆਰੁ ॥
lines.translit: vin preetee bhagat na hovee vin satigur na lagai piaar |
lines.english: Without love, there is no devotion. Without the True Guru, love is not enshrined.

lines.gurmukhi: ਗੁਰ ਕੈ ਸਬਦਿ ਸਲਾਹੀਐ ਅੰਤਰਿ ਪ੍ਰੇਮ ਪਿਆਰੁ ॥
lines.translit: gur kai sabad salaaheeai antar prem piaar |
lines.english: Through the Word of the Guru's Shabad, praise the Lord with love and affection within.

lines.gurmukhi: ਦੇਹਿ ਦਾਨੁ ਸੰਤੋਖੀਆ ਸਚਾ ਨਾਮੁ ਮਿਲੈ ਆਧਾਰੁ ॥੧੯॥
lines.translit: dehi daan santokheea sachaa naam milai aadhaar |19|
lines.english: Please bless me with the gift of contentment, that I may receive the True Name as my Support. ||19||

lines.gurmukhi: ਤੂ ਪ੍ਰਭੁ ਸਭਿ ਤੁਧੁ ਸੇਵਦੇ ਇਕ ਢਾਢੀ ਕਰੇ ਪੁਕਾਰ ॥
lines.translit: too prabh sabh tudh sevade ik dtaadtee kare pukaar |
lines.english: You are the Lord God; everyone serves You. This is the prayer of Your humble minstrel.
